comment.body:
know how much money you can put into work, 
what risk you can take that day.
place your bets based on your read, and watch for the dynamics to prove or disprove your bets.

Cut the losers quickly, when disproven, size up on the winners, and jump quick out.
If all of your positions end up in loss, don't fomo.

it happens. 
you weren't wrong. Market works that way. Probability  works that way.
Never take it personal!

comment.body:
so what i wrote in the weekly post is basically this:

1) monthly positioning is short vanna = vol up is squeezish, vol down is suppressive (in simple terms)
2) vomma supply = any vol spikes is doomed to be sold. so vol is moderated anyway.
3) based on momentum, vol some negative imbalance, that it will rebalance at some point.
A vol spike would create a blowoff top.. 6350/60 (me personally don't see 6400 now)
4) but the vol spike would be sold... now... here is the twist:
- the positioning has long zomma = so vol up increases the gammas even in the shorter-term tenors. This means, that into the July OpEx that is a long speed (short gamma down, long gamma up), positioning, the vol effect would leave increased upside gamma behind.
- depending on the spot the market is trading at, it can be a) stabilizing, b) suppressive. 
(both is possible, i don't see the levels yet)

5) after the gamma unclench occurs, vol can grind slowly higher.
this would make the market a) either ranging, if it is hgih enough, b) slowly grinding down, in februar stlye

a simple RSI is a good invention.
sometimes it can help. not a holy grail, but it is usefull for technicals.

it is simply the (green candles)/(red candles) over the past period you set.

and if the buying activity increases, the ratio will increase, as the numerator increases faster relative to the denominator.

Question for you Alma, what about methods that are kind of in between traditional technicals and things like higher levels quant math? I’ve studied JM Hurst’s work a ton and it’s centered around frequency and amplitude analysis (spectral and phasing analysis with filters). comment.body:
This overfitting question... 
Yeah, there is no exact answer for that. Be careful, bcs scientists as well, try to formalize everything, hoping there is still free lunch somewhere . This is a wrong approach and the purpose of science is different. 
It is all acreative process.
Anyways... 

You have to know whats the purpose, and how the formulas, metrics work, what they do to know what you can expect from them and what not. 

For me, it is making rough probability distributions from historical datas on vol and the using this, i meassure the momentum of the price action, and use the vol paths, to make a price distribution. 
Keep it simple. 

By meassuring the momentum, it is all about to check whether a momentum is accelerating or deccelerating. 
This is what you try to project from the past datas, bcs if the will is there, there must be signs. And thats the purpose of all of those models. To reveal these tendencies.
